channel.title: UART on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink
channel.description: Recent content in UART on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink

item.title: UART Break/Autobaud
item.description:
Microchip UART modules support advanced communication features including Break character transmission/detection and Auto-Baud rate detection for robust communication in systems where baud rates are unknown or need synchronization.

Overview

Microchip UART modules support advanced communication features including Break character transmission/detection and Auto-Baud rate detection. These features enable robust communication in systems where baud rates are unknown or need synchronization, and provide special signaling capabilities for protocol implementations.

item.title: UART Config Block
item.description:
UART_Config Block The UART Config block configures a UART (Universal Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter) module for serial communication. This block sets up baud rate, pin assignments, operating modes, and implementation methods for both transmit and receive operations.

When to use:

  • Serial communication setup — Configure UART before using UART Tx/Rx blocks
  • Multi-UART systems — Set up multiple UART modules with different baud rates
  • External Mode & PIL — Enable XCP communication for Simulink External Mode
  • High-throughput serial — Configure DMA or circular buffers for reliable data transfer
  • Hardware flow control — Enable RTS/CTS for robust RS-232 communication

When NOT to use:

item.title: UART Rx Block
item.description:
UART_Rx Block Icon The UART Rx block receives data from a configured UART module. This block outputs 8-bit data (uint8 type) with flexible size configuration including inherited sizing, user-defined vectors, and automatic buffer sizing based on baud rate and sample time. It works in conjunction with the UART Config block to provide efficient serial reception.

When to use:

  • Receive serial data — Get bytes from UART into Simulink signals
  • String reception — Receive text commands or messages (use “Add trailing 0” mode)
  • Binary protocols — Receive packets, sensor data, or command structures
  • Buffered reception — Use FIFO/DMA modes for reliable high-speed reception
  • Multi-device communication — Receive from multiple UART peripherals (one Rx block per UART)

When NOT to use:

item.title: UART Tx Block
item.description:
UART_Tx Block Icon The UART Tx block transmits data through a configured UART module. This block sends 8-bit data (uint8 type) and supports variable-length transmission with optional feedback on bytes sent. It works in conjunction with the UART Config block to provide flexible, efficient serial transmission.

When to use:

  • Send serial data — Transmit bytes from Simulink to external devices
  • Command/response protocols — Send control commands, status messages, or responses
  • Binary data streaming — Send sensor data, telemetry, or binary protocols
  • Variable-length packets — Use N input for dynamic packet sizes
  • High-throughput transmission — Use DMA ping-pong for continuous streaming

When NOT to use:

item.title: UART TxMatlab Block
item.description:
UART_TxMatlab Block Icon The UART TxMatlab block transmits numerical data through UART to MATLAB (or alternative hosts) for real-time monitoring, plotting, and recording. This block supports multiple data types (int8, uint8, int16, uint16, int32, uint32, single, double) and implements a packetized protocol for reliable data streaming and visualization using the picgui MATLAB interface.

When to use:

  • Real-time monitoring — Stream sensor data, motor parameters, or control signals to MATLAB
  • picgui visualization — Plot channels in real-time using picgui interface
  • Data logging — Record execution data for offline analysis
  • Debug signals — Monitor internal states during development
  • Multi-channel streaming — Send up to 16 channels simultaneously

When NOT to use:

item.title: MCHP picgui - UART Graphical Interface
item.description:
Launch MATLAB graphical interface for interactive UART communication

Overview

The picgui block provides a convenient launcher for the MATLAB-based graphical user interface that enables interactive UART communication with Microchip devices. This GUI tool allows you to send and receive UART data interactively during development and debugging.

Block Dialog

The picgui block itself has an empty Simulink mask — double-clicking it launches the MATLAB GUI shown below. Three illustrations, from most didactic to most realistic:
